title: Zomato Restaurant Scraper
description: Pick a city, optionally a cuisine, and how many restaurants to scrape. Results include name, phone, address, cuisines, rating, and cost for two.

city.title: City
city.description: Pick the Zomato city to scrape.

listingType.title: Listing Type
listingType.description: Which list of restaurants do you want?

cuisine.title: Cuisine (optional)
cuisine.description: Filter by cuisine. Leave on "Any" for no filter.

maxRestaurants.title: How many restaurants?
maxRestaurants.description: Cap on results per scan. Start small (20-50) and increase as needed.

scanDepth.title: Scan depth
scanDepth.description: Quick = restaurant URLs + names only (fast, cheap). Standard = full data (phone, address, rating, cost). Deep = full data with extra retries for blocked pages.
enumTitles: Deep (full data + retries for blocked pages)

customCity.title: Custom city (overrides City dropdown)
customCity.description: City name not in the dropdown? Type it here. Examples: 'Erode', 'Tirunelveli', 'Coorg', 'Manali', 'Madurai', 'Varanasi'. Auto-converted to slug (lowercase + dashes). Tip: Zomato uses CITY URLs only — there's no state-level URL, so 'Tamil Nadu' won't work; pick a city like 'Madurai' or 'Coimbatore'.
customCity.sectionDescription: Optional escape hatches when the dropdowns above don't have what you want.

customCuisine.title: Custom cuisine (overrides Cuisine dropdown)
customCuisine.description: Cuisine not in the dropdown? Type it here. Examples that work: 'Momos', 'Kebab', 'Hyderabadi', 'Awadhi'. Auto-converted to slug. Note: Zomato only has CUISINE CATEGORIES — specific dishes like 'Dahi Puri' or 'Tandoor' won't have their own URL (those aren't cuisine categories). For dish-level searches, use a broader category like 'Street Food' or 'North Indian'.

customUrl.title: Custom URL (full override)
customUrl.description: Paste any Zomato listing URL. Overrides every dropdown + custom field above. Example: https://www.zomato.com/dehradun/restaurants/biryani.

timeoutSeconds.title: Run timeout (seconds)
timeoutSeconds.description: Hard kill the scan after this many seconds. Default 1800 (30 min) handles most multi-city scans.
